title: Python, Tcl, Tkinter, Threads, and the Event Loop · Python Build Room
summary: A museum puzzle team incrementally builds single-root event-loop lifecycle contract from original seeded fixtures while retaining earlier Python, graph, determinism, accessibility, testing, performance, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of single-root event-loop lifecycle contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.
Create one application root, initialize state before mainloop, and shut down widgets and callbacks deliberately.
Keep event handlers short and divide long generation or search work into bounded event-loop turns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Python Tcl Tk Layers
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop puzzle team handles case 5126 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace Python calls through tkinter into one Tcl interpreter and the Tk widget toolkit with recorded patch levels.

For runtime evidence · changed case, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Runtime Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-runtime-evidence-122jd0" that uses case 8298 to model a single-root cooperative event loop with bounded callbacks and deliberate shutdown. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Runtime Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Runtime Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Runtime Evidence, keep Runtime Evidence connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-runtime-evidence-122jd0
# Competency: maze-runtime-evidence.
# Case 8298: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_runtime_evidence_122jd0(roots: int = 1, pending_callbacks: int = 0, longest_callback_ms: int = 7, ui_thread_owned: bool = True, clean_shutdown: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_122jd0 = "122jd0-0dj221-8298"
    assert evidence_variant_122jd0
    evidence_axes_122jd0 = (
        "grid122jd0",
        "topology8298",
        "search0dj221",
        "event122jd08298",
        "access8298122jd0",
        "recovery0dj2218298",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_122jd0)) == 6
    if roots != 1:
        return False, roots, "root-lifecycle"
    if pending_callbacks < 0:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "callback-count"
    if longest_callback_ms < 0 or longest_callback_ms > 16:
        return False, longest_callback_ms, "blocking-callback"
    if not ui_thread_owned or not clean_shutdown:
        return False, pending_callbacks, "runtime-ownership"
    assert roots == 1 and pending_callbacks == 0
    return True, longest_callback_ms, "cooperative-runtime"
